respondent.notes: Sample response from initial test group

responses.text: My faith. It shapes my values, guides my decisions, and gives me a sense of purpose and direction every day.

I admire people who are confident without being arrogant—those who can admit when they don't know something and are comfortable not having all the answers. I'd like to have that same balance of confidence, humility, and curiosity myself.

items: I want to trust myself more at work and stop second-guessing my decisions.
I want to be more understanding and patient with my family and husband.
I want to read more, grow in my faith, and make time for the things that are truly important, not just work.

responses.text: I have no control over the choices and actions of other people, the unexpected events that happen in life, and the pressures of society to go along with things I don't believe in. While I can't control these, I can control how I respond and the example I set through the way I live my life.

One influence I feel I have under control is my work. I know what I am doing, and when I'm unsure, I have the support of people who genuinely want the best for everyone. Even when things change, I am confident in managing my responsibilities effectively. I also feel I have control over how I view my life. I focus on what I have rather than what I lack, and I feel content, knowing I have everything I need and want for nothing.

items: Faith – Believing in something greater than ourselves.
Selflessness – Acting with care and consideration for others.
Empathy – Recognizing that, by the grace of God, I could be in someone else's place.
My tendency to second-guess myself at work
My desire for approval from my family
The habits or impulses I sometimes struggle to manage
Ask questions instead of assuming you know all the answers. Listen more and speak less; you don't have to be the loudest person in the room to be the most effective.

Learn from those who do things well and don't be afraid to ask for help—growth happens faster when you don't try to go it alone. And it's ok to make mistakes; they're one of the best ways to learn and improve.
Faith and gratitude
Love and compassion for my family
Living with integrity, empathy, and grace
Purposely hurt someone physically or emotionally
Do something illegal or unethical
Compromise my faith
Raise children with strong values and beliefs
Mentor or guide others
Perform regular acts of kindness
Share knowledge and wisdom
Lead with integrity and empathy
Build meaningful relationships
Feed people in need
Fund education and missionaries
Support housing and shelters for the homeless
Provide life experiences for children and underserved communities
Help orphanages and children's homes with essentials
Fund healthcare and medical aid

responses.text: Yes. All in question 15. 1st 2 in question 16.

items: My faith in God
Acting with integrity and staying true to my values
Showing love and compassion to my family and others
Practicing gratitude for the blessings in my life
Committing to continuous growth
Treating people and situations with empathy and understanding
Gives me purpose and guidance; it helps me stay grounded and make decisions
Ensures I act honestly and responsibly, building trust with others and keeping myself accountable
Shapes how I interact with my family and others, fostering stronger, healthier relationships
Keeps me focused on what I have rather than what I lack, helping me stay positive and content
Pushes me to learn from experiences, improve myself and allows me to understand others

responses.text: Good. 3 reasons: I have a great team around me who believe in me and what we are working towards. We have the same goals with purpose. This motivates me to do my best. Second, we all want the same things and share one common goal, which keeps us focused and aligned to the core values. We all want each other to be the best versions of themselves. And third, I have the clarity and focus to prioritize what matters most, which helps me make meaningful progress.
Yes: I am just like everyone else, and we should all do our part no matter how small to help those in need. We can all make a difference.

belief_response.why: How can there be a creation without a Creator. I can't not believe. 9301.
belief_response.advantages: Peace, security, love, compassion, hope, being a part of something, forgiveness.
belief_response.evidence: The Bible. It's never been disproven. Documented accounts of Jesus.
